title: scorzonera tau-saghyz subsp. mariae
intro: Scorzonera tau-saghyz subsp. mariae, also known as the Russian dandelion or Turkestan dandelion, is a perennial plant native to Central Asia, belonging to the sunflower family (Asteraceae). It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and diuretic effects, which have been shown to help alleviate symptoms of various health conditions. The plant's leaves and roots are rich in vitamins, minerals, and flavonoids, offering potential health benefits such as reducing blood pressure, improving digestion, and protecting against oxidative stress. Scorzonera tau-saghyz subsp. mariae can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, tinctures, and capsules, allowing for convenient and accessible use in traditional and modern medicine.
